rationale: The citing paper explicitly discusses Ariely, Huber, and Wertenbroch (2005) in its conceptual background on emotions, stating that emotional attachment is a key mechanism behind variation in the endowment effect. This citation is used to support the paper's emotion-based framing for why losses can lead to higher-than-planned subsequent betting. Although the cited work is not methodologically reused, it meaningfully informs hypothesis motivation and theoretical positioning rather than serving as a mere passing mention.
relation_description: The citing paper draws on Ariely et al. (2005) as conceptual evidence that emotional attachment can amplify or mitigate loss-related valuation effects. It uses that logic to ground its argument that underestimated negative emotions after losses drive upward deviations from planned bets.
